findings.description: VulnerableBank.isLucky() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#80-83) uses a weak PRNG: "block.timestamp % 7 == 0 (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#82)"

Reentrancy in VulnerableBank.withdraw() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#40-52):
	External calls:
	- (success,None) = msg.sender.call{value: balance}() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#45)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- balances[msg.sender] = 0 (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#49)
	VulnerableBank.balances (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#16) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- VulnerableBank.balances (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#16)
	- VulnerableBank.deposit() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#29-33)
	- VulnerableBank.getBalance(address) (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#88-90)
	- VulnerableBank.withdraw() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#40-52)

VulnerableBank.isLucky() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#80-83) uses a dangerous strict equality:
	- block.timestamp % 7 == 0 (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#82)
VulnerableBank.emergencyWithdraw(address,uint256) (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#68-73) ignores return value by recipient.call{value: amount}() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#72)

VulnerableBank.setOwner(address).newOwner (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#59) lacks a zero-check on :
		- owner = newOwner (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#61)

VulnerableBank.emergencyWithdraw(address,uint256).recipient (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#68) lacks a zero-check on :
		- recipient.call{value: amount}() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#72)

Reentrancy in VulnerableBank.withdraw() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#40-52):
	External calls:
	- (success,None) = msg.sender.call{value: balance}() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#45)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- Withdrawal(msg.sender,balance) (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#51)

VulnerableBank.isLucky() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#80-83) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- block.timestamp % 7 == 0 (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#82)

Version constraint ^0.8.0 contains known severe issues (https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/latest/bugs.html)
	- FullInlinerNonExpressionSplitArgumentEvaluationOrder
	- MissingSideEffectsOnSelectorAccess
	- AbiReencodingHeadOverflowWithStaticArrayCleanup
	- DirtyBytesArrayToStorage
	- DataLocationChangeInInternalOverride
	- NestedCalldataArrayAbiReencodingSizeValidation
	- SignedImmutables
	- ABIDecodeTwoDimensionalArrayMemory
	- KeccakCaching.
It is used by:
	- ^0.8.0 (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#2)

Low level call in VulnerableBank.withdraw() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#40-52):
	- (success,None) = msg.sender.call{value: balance}() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#45)

Low level call in VulnerableBank.emergencyWithdraw(address,uint256) (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#68-73):
	- recipient.call{value: amount}() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#72)
Attacker.bank (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#109) should be immutable

Reentrancy in VulnerableBank.withdraw() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#40-52):
	External calls:
	- (success,None) = msg.sender.call{value: balance}() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#45)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- balances[msg.sender] = 0 (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#49)
	VulnerableBank.balances (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#16) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- VulnerableBank.balances (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#16)
	- VulnerableBank.deposit() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#29-33)
	- VulnerableBank.getBalance(address) (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#88-90)
	- VulnerableBank.withdraw() (examples/vulnerable_bank.sol#40-52)
findings.recommendation: Manual review recommended (GPT analysis not available)
